features.title: Product Constitution, Semantic Sovereignty, and Autonomous Authority
Visual Asset Demand Contract, Intake, and Authority Validation
Canonical Asset Ontology and Reference/Production Separation
Immutable Asset Lifecycle, Lineage, Supersession, and Delivery Variants
Composition Intent, Feasibility, and Image-Conditioned Geometry
Governed Multi-Method Resolution and Strategy Routing
Dynamic Specialist Workcell and Authority Boundaries
Visual Capability Registry for Workflows, Models, LoRAs, Controls, and Runtimes
Visual Production Plan IR and Provider-Specific Compilation
Event-Sourced, Resumable Visual Production Runtime
Visual Asset Memory, Syntax-Aware Reuse, and Contextual Recurrence
Hybrid Containerized Visual Compute Fabric
Governed LoRA, Adapter, Control, and Workflow Capability Development
Visual Evaluation Profiles and Independent VLM Quality System
Typed Visual Repair, Invalidation, and Bounded Reruns
Budget Programs, Candidate Portfolios, and Quality-First Selection
Visual Steering Intelligence, CMF-OKF Knowledge, and Smart Retrieval
Control Tower Specialization and Supervisory Console
Asynchronous Visual Asset Service and Delegation Boundary
Constraint Conflicts, Feasibility Evidence, and Amendment Proposals
Benchmark Portfolio, Staged Certification, and Release 1 Format 02 Slice
Independent Versioning, Architecture Preservation, Readiness, and Development Capsule

functional_requirements.title: Enforce the complete visual asset-resolution promise
functional_requirements.requirement: The service must accept an authorized Visual Asset Demand and resolve it through any approved combination of reuse, retrieval, extraction, transformation, compositing, deterministic construction, generation, animation, or capture request.
testable_consequences: A run may select and combine only routes registered as eligible for the requested family, role, and demand.
A system limited to a single generation or manipulation route fails product-conformance validation.

functional_requirements.title: Preserve semantic sovereignty
functional_requirements.requirement: No production node, specialist, memory result, workflow fallback, evaluator, repair, or operator action may change the authorized semantic intent, Activative function, sequence role, composition role, identity, continuity, or wrong-reading locks in place.
testable_consequences: Every accepted asset can be traced to unchanged authoritative demand fields or to a newer explicitly authorized demand version.
Any silent substitution produces a constitutional hard-gate failure and blocks asset promotion.

functional_requirements.title: Operate autonomously inside authorized boundaries
functional_requirements.requirement: Routine demand validation, planning, memory retrieval, capability selection, candidate production, evaluation, repair, promotion, and delivery must execute without manual intervention.
testable_consequences: A fully passing routine run reaches COMPLETED without an approval click, manual prompt edit, candidate selection, or job restart.
A workflow that pauses for ordinary operator approval fails the no-routine-manual-work acceptance test.

functional_requirements.title: Limit human intervention to typed exceptions
functional_requirements.requirement: Human review may be requested only for max-repair exhaustion, unauthorized cost, unresolved capability gaps, blocking constraint conflicts, unresolved evaluator contradiction, or explicit degraded-result authority.
testable_consequences: Every human intervention contains a typed trigger, evidence, attempted repairs, preserved state, choices, and consequences.
Unstructured requests such as 'please review' without an authorized exception code are rejected.

functional_requirements.title: Apply three acceptance gates
functional_requirements.requirement: An asset is accepted only after production validity, demand fidelity, and intended-composition effectiveness pass their required profiles.
testable_consequences: The final result receipt contains separate verdicts and evidence for all three gates.
A high aggregate score cannot compensate for failure in any mandatory gate.

functional_requirements.title: Separate human authority from production execution
functional_requirements.requirement: The operator may govern budgets, production priorities, experimental scope, cost ceilings, exception choices, and capability promotion, but does not normally manipulate ComfyUI graphs or generation parameters.
testable_consequences: The supervisory console exposes policy and exception controls while normal runs retain autonomous node execution.
A production path requiring routine node-level operator work is outside certified scope.

functional_requirements.title: Keep lightweight source provenance without a rights workcell
functional_requirements.requirement: Source type, origin reference, allowed-source policy result, generated/retrieved/supplied classification, and transformation lineage must be captured deterministically without a standalone Rights Analyst.
testable_consequences: Every delivered asset contains provenance metadata sufficient to reconstruct its source class and derivation.
Missing provenance blocks promotion, but routine production does not enter a manual legal-review lane.

functional_requirements.title: Authorize Architecture before implementation
functional_requirements.requirement: Approval of this PRD permits Architecture work only; implementation begins after the formal Visual Asset Editor Implementation Authorization Gate passes.
testable_consequences: The readiness receipt identifies the exact evidence, contracts, reference slice, compute proof, evaluators, benchmarks, and Development Capsule used for authorization.
A PRD-approved but architecture-unvalidated package cannot receive IMPLEMENTATION_AUTHORIZED.

functional_requirements.title: Require a typed Visual Asset Demand
functional_requirements.requirement: Every production request must reference a schema-valid, versioned Visual Asset Demand containing asset family, harness role, Activative function, semantic intent, composition intent, continuity, wrong-reading locks, delivery, evaluation, and execution-policy fields applicable to the request.
testable_consequences: Contract validation can enumerate every missing or invalid field before plan compilation.
Natural-language-only requests are rejected with INVALID_DEMAND.

functional_requirements.title: Treat notes as non-authoritative enrichment
functional_requirements.requirement: Optional notes may explain preferences or context but must be tagged as untrusted enrichment and cannot override typed fields, hard gates, budgets, or authority.
testable_consequences: A conflict detector reports notes that contradict the typed demand and preserves the typed value.
A note containing prompt-like instructions cannot alter workflow, tool, or evaluator policy.

functional_requirements.title: Version demands immutably
functional_requirements.requirement: Once accepted for execution, a Visual Asset Demand version is immutable; material changes require a new version with supersession and amendment provenance.
testable_consequences: Each execution pins one exact demand version and retains it after completion.
In-place edits to an accepted demand fail contract-authority validation.

functional_requirements.title: Validate caller identity and delegation authority
functional_requirements.requirement: Only registered Content Harnesses, orchestration systems, or authorized operators may submit, cancel, choose high-cost programs, enable experimental capability, accept degradation, or request capability development.
testable_consequences: Submission receipts record caller identity, scope, and permitted actions.
An unauthorized action returns a typed authorization failure without starting production.

functional_requirements.title: Use idempotent submission keys
functional_requirements.requirement: The asynchronous service must require or derive an idempotency key from caller, harness, request ID, and demand version.
testable_consequences: Repeated identical submissions return the existing execution record rather than duplicating GPU work.
A changed demand with a reused key is rejected as an idempotency conflict.

functional_requirements.title: Reference large inputs by stable URI and hash
functional_requirements.requirement: Images, videos, masks, character references, composition renders, and other large inputs must remain in governed storage and enter contracts through versioned references and content hashes.
testable_consequences: The intake validator proves referenced objects exist, are readable, and match declared hashes.
Embedded unbounded media payloads or hash mismatches block acceptance.

functional_requirements.title: Return a typed submission receipt
functional_requirements.requirement: A valid submission must immediately return execution identity, accepted demand version, Budget Program, initial status, estimate class, status resource, and event endpoint information.
testable_consequences: The caller can begin observing a run without waiting for GPU completion.
An HTTP success without a durable execution and receipt is nonconformant.

functional_requirements.title: Block unresolved authority and dependency gaps
functional_requirements.requirement: Intake or pre-plan validation must identify missing upstream authority, unavailable required references, unsupported category/profile combinations, and contradictory hard constraints before production resources are committed.
testable_consequences: The service emits a typed blocker with responsible owner and next action.
The system may not invent missing semantic or composition values to make a demand executable.

functional_requirements.title: Maintain eight canonical asset families
functional_requirements.requirement: The ontology must initially define documentary/photographic evidence, human/character assets, illustrated/generated scenes, UI/screen surfaces, diagrammatic/informational assets, typography/graphic elements, compositing/scene components, and motion/temporal assets.
testable_consequences: Every demand selects one registered family and compatible subtype.
Unknown families cannot route to production until governed ontology extension.

functional_requirements.title: Bind harness-specific roles and Activative functions
functional_requirements.requirement: A canonical family record must be combined with a requesting harness role, sequence or scene position, visual-syntax function, and Activative function.
testable_consequences: Two demands may share an asset family while retaining distinct semantic and composition roles.
The ontology may not replace or generalize away the harness-specific role.

functional_requirements.title: Represent Reference Evidence explicitly
functional_requirements.requirement: Material used for understanding, conditioning, identity, pose, environment, composition, continuity, evaluation, or inspiration must be stored as Reference Evidence unless explicitly promoted.
testable_consequences: Reference records state relationships to demands and production plans.
Reference Evidence cannot be emitted as an authorized final asset by default.

functional_requirements.title: Represent Production Assets explicitly
functional_requirements.requirement: A Production Asset must be an immutable, evaluated artifact authorized for one or more declared downstream uses and linked to its demand, plan, lineage, geometry, and receipts.
testable_consequences: Downstream composition can verify production authorization from the asset record.
An unpromoted candidate or reference cannot satisfy an Asset Result Contract.

functional_requirements.title: Require explicit reference-to-production promotion
functional_requirements.requirement: When a reference is reused or transformed into production, the system must record the promotion event, transformation, evaluator results, source policy, and new immutable Production Asset identity.
testable_consequences: Promotion preserves the original reference record and creates a distinct production lineage.
Folder movement or filename change alone cannot promote an asset.

functional_requirements.title: Publish subtype capability requirements
functional_requirements.requirement: Each subtype must declare eligible routes, required controls, compatible workflows/models, evaluator dimensions, geometry needs, continuity rules, and delivery types.
testable_consequences: The router can determine whether certified capabilities exist before plan execution.
A subtype without capability requirements remains structurally represented but uncertified.

functional_requirements.title: Version and migrate ontology records
functional_requirements.requirement: Asset-family and subtype changes must be versioned, compatibility-scoped, and migration-tested against dependent demands, plans, memory records, and results.
testable_consequences: Existing assets preserve the ontology version used at production time.
A taxonomy rename cannot silently reinterpret historical records.

functional_requirements.title: Expose certified scope separately from represented scope
functional_requirements.requirement: The ontology and UI must mark each family/subtype as represented, experimental, limited-production, production-certified, deprecated, or retired.
testable_consequences: Release 1 reports Format 02 character-and-scene capabilities as certified scope only after benchmarks pass.
Structurally defined families cannot be marketed or routed as production-ready.

functional_requirements.title: Create immutable asset versions
functional_requirements.requirement: Every material candidate, repair, composite, accepted master, or meaningfully changed output must receive an immutable asset ID/version and content hash.
testable_consequences: A version can be reproduced or audited after later repairs and supersession.
Overwriting asset bytes under an existing accepted version is prohibited.

functional_requirements.title: Enforce explicit lifecycle transitions
functional_requirements.requirement: Asset state changes must follow registered transitions from Reference Evidence or Resolution Planned through Candidate, Evaluation, Accepted Production Asset, Delivery Variant, and Consumed states, with typed failure states.
testable_consequences: Illegal transitions are rejected and emitted to the Control Tower.
A rejected candidate cannot jump directly to accepted without new evaluation.

functional_requirements.title: Record typed derivation relationships
functional_requirements.requirement: Every version must declare parents and relationships such as generated-from, extracted-from, transformed-from, composited-from, targeted-repair-of, delivery-variant-of, or supersedes.
testable_consequences: Lineage traversal reconstructs all sources and production steps.
An accepted asset with an orphaned material parent fails promotion.

functional_requirements.title: Separate accepted masters from delivery variants
functional_requirements.requirement: The accepted master is the canonical production asset; deterministic crops, codecs, masks, previews, thumbnails, and profile-specific outputs are immutable variants linked to it.
testable_consequences: Consumers can request a declared variant without changing master identity.
A variant cannot silently become the new master.

functional_requirements.title: Notify downstream consumers of supersession
functional_requirements.requirement: When a new accepted version supersedes another, in-progress dependent compositions receive an event and revalidation requirement while published content remains bound to its historical version.
testable_consequences: The system can list all affected in-progress and completed consumers.
Supersession may not mutate already-published artifacts automatically.

functional_requirements.title: Preserve evaluation and production receipts by version
functional_requirements.requirement: Each asset version must reference its exact plan, workflow, compute, candidate, geometry, evaluator, repair, budget, and acceptance receipts.
testable_consequences: A reviewer can prove why one version passed and another failed.
Receipts from a parent version cannot be reused as acceptance evidence for an altered child without revalidation.

functional_requirements.title: Support deterministic reuse of valid variants
functional_requirements.requirement: Repeated requests for an existing valid delivery profile may return the stored immutable variant when source master, profile version, and dependencies are unchanged.
testable_consequences: Idempotent variant generation avoids duplicate transformations.
A profile-version change creates and evaluates a new variant rather than overwriting the old one.

functional_requirements.title: Expose lifecycle and lineage in the Control Tower
functional_requirements.requirement: The supervisory console must render asset state, versions, parents, repairs, accepted master, variants, usage, supersession, and pending invalidations.
testable_consequences: Operators can navigate from a result contract to every contributing artifact and receipt.
A lifecycle status shown without authoritative event or record is invalid.

functional_requirements.title: Require typed Composition Intent
functional_requirements.requirement: Applicable demands must declare canvas profile, intended region, tolerance, layer, visual weight, depth, directional constraints, reserved regions, crop policy, protected regions, and background policy.
testable_consequences: The plan compiler can map each composition requirement to production and evaluation nodes.
Ambiguous prose such as 'leave room for text' is insufficient when a typed field exists.

functional_requirements.title: Run composition-feasibility analysis before expensive production
functional_requirements.requirement: The system must test whether subject count, camera distance, gesture, gaze, protected regions, text reservations, and intended BBOX can coexist with available capabilities and budget.
testable_consequences: Blocking conflicts are detected before full candidate generation when simulation can establish infeasibility.
The system may not consume the full candidate budget for a geometrically impossible plan.

functional_requirements.title: Compile composition controls from intent
functional_requirements.requirement: The production plan must translate composition intent into masks, regional conditioning, pose/depth/edge controls, camera constraints, background extension, transparency, and workflow-specific bindings as supported.
testable_consequences: Each compiled control traces to one demand field or feasibility result.
Provider parameters without a governing composition reason are rejected from the canonical binding.

functional_requirements.title: Return image-conditioned geometry
functional_requirements.requirement: Accepted assets must return detected subject, face, hands, gesture, object, and focal BBOXes; gaze or motion vectors; negative-space regions; safe crops; masks; depth/layer data; and collision results as applicable.
testable_consequences: Downstream composition can validate fit without rerunning the full production model.
An asset requiring geometry that lacks the applicable geometry pack cannot be authorized for composition.

functional_requirements.title: Respect composition tolerance
functional_requirements.requirement: The editor may adjust geometry only within declared tolerance or through an internal production method that preserves the requested role and function.
testable_consequences: The result contract records requested versus realized geometry and tolerance consumption.
A change outside tolerance triggers a demand amendment proposal rather than silent relocation.

functional_requirements.title: Evaluate assets in rendered composition context
functional_requirements.requirement: The evaluation graph must place candidates in the target scene, slide, or frame with text and neighboring elements before composition-effectiveness approval.
testable_consequences: Evaluation evidence includes the rendered composition hash and detected collisions or hierarchy outcomes.
Standalone asset quality cannot substitute for composition-context evaluation.

functional_requirements.title: Generate deterministic delivery geometry variants
functional_requirements.requirement: After master acceptance, the system may derive registered crop, mask, transparency, and canvas variants and return each with inherited geometry and fresh profile validation.
testable_consequences: Delivery variants retain protected regions and focal visibility.
A crop that removes the required action or subject evidence fails variant authorization.

functional_requirements.title: Return typed composition conflicts and recommendations
functional_requirements.requirement: When requirements remain infeasible after approved repairs and fallbacks, the system must return conflict evidence and nonbinding geometry or route alternatives to the owning Content Harness.
testable_consequences: The original demand remains immutable and each option states semantic and Activative impact.
The editor cannot accept its own out-of-tolerance amendment.

functional_requirements.title: Register canonical production routes
functional_requirements.requirement: The capability registry must define reuse, retrieve, extract, transform, composite, deterministic construction, generate, animate, and request-capture routes with inputs, outputs, eligible families, required capabilities, evaluators, and certification status.
testable_consequences: The router can validate route eligibility before planning.
An unregistered route cannot execute in production.

functional_requirements.title: Select the least complex reliable route
functional_requirements.requirement: Strategy selection must rank eligible routes by expected semantic fidelity, Activative effectiveness, composition fit, continuity, feasibility, latency, cost, repairability, and reproducibility.
testable_consequences: The plan records selected and rejected alternatives with evidence.
Generation-first selection without comparison fails routing evaluation when a simpler certified route can satisfy the demand.

functional_requirements.title: Support governed hybrid routes
functional_requirements.requirement: A production plan may sequence multiple routes when one route cannot satisfy the demand alone, such as retrieval plus extraction plus transformation plus compositing.
testable_consequences: Every stage has typed contracts, dependencies, validators, and lineage relationships.
A hybrid plan cannot hide an unsupported or uncertified stage.

functional_requirements.title: Prefer governed asset reuse when suitable
functional_requirements.requirement: Before acquiring or generating new material, the system must query Visual Asset Memory for semantically, Activatively, compositionally, and continuity-compatible accepted assets and account for contextual recurrence.
testable_consequences: The reuse decision includes suitability and recurrence evidence.
Low raw similarity alone cannot exclude a strong syntax-context match, and high similarity alone cannot authorize a poor role match.

functional_requirements.title: Prepare and validate source/control inputs
functional_requirements.requirement: Retrieval, extraction, generation, animation, and compositing routes must normalize references, masks, pose/depth maps, identity inputs, environments, documents, frames, and control assets before downstream execution.
testable_consequences: Prepared inputs receive hashes, geometry, class, and validation status.
A production node cannot consume unvalidated or mismatched control inputs.

functional_requirements.title: Define route-specific fallback policies
functional_requirements.requirement: Each route profile must declare fallback routes, compatibility constraints, quality impact, budget impact, and conditions that prohibit fallback.
testable_consequences: Infrastructure or capability failure can select an approved alternative without changing demand authority.
A fallback that weakens a hard semantic or composition requirement must return a conflict instead.

functional_requirements.title: Emit route and strategy receipts
functional_requirements.requirement: Every run must preserve route candidates, selected strategy, expected and actual costs, capability bindings, fallbacks used, and reasons for route changes.
testable_consequences: Control Tower views can explain why a route was chosen and whether it changed during repair.
An accepted asset without a reconstructable resolution strategy is nonconformant.

functional_requirements.title: Learn route effectiveness without self-modifying silently
functional_requirements.requirement: Cross-run evidence may update routing statistics and propose Steering Recipes or profile changes only through the governed learning lifecycle.
testable_consequences: The system can compare expected and realized route performance by family and syntax context.
One successful or failed run cannot rewrite route defaults automatically.

functional_requirements.title: Register specialist authorities
functional_requirements.requirement: The product must define Asset Intelligence Hunter, Activative Visual Analyst, Composition Feasibility Analyst, Resolution Strategy Composer, Editor/Materializer, Independent Visual Evaluator, and Asset Commander authority manifests.
testable_consequences: Each manifest declares owned decisions, inputs, outputs, tools, prohibited decisions, maturity, and evaluation profile.
A specialist cannot act outside its declared authority.

functional_requirements.title: Compile the smallest sufficient workcell
functional_requirements.requirement: The Asset Commander must activate specialists based on demand, route, capability, evaluation, and repair needs rather than run every specialist for every request.
testable_consequences: A simple crop may skip research and generation; a new character scene activates the required analysis, plan, production, and evaluation authorities.
Unnecessary specialist activation is visible in cost and workflow tests.

functional_requirements.title: Keep semantic interpretation bounded
functional_requirements.requirement: The Activative Visual Analyst may translate authorized intent into production-facing visible requirements and failure indicators but may not change the demand.
testable_consequences: Its output traces each production-facing requirement to a demand field.
A newly invented subject, message, sequence role, or wrong-reading rule is rejected.

functional_requirements.title: Keep feasibility analysis measurable
functional_requirements.requirement: The Composition Feasibility Analyst must output geometry constraints, conflicts, tolerances, supported controls, and evidence rather than aesthetic preference alone.
testable_consequences: Its result can be validated against simulation or candidate geometry.
An unsupported qualitative feasibility conclusion cannot block or redirect production.

functional_requirements.title: Separate strategy from materialization
functional_requirements.requirement: The Resolution Strategy Composer owns the typed route and capability plan; Editor/Materializer nodes execute the approved plan and cannot independently redesign it.
testable_consequences: Execution receipts prove which plan and bindings were materialized.
Materializer-side changes beyond permitted runtime variation require a plan amendment.

functional_requirements.title: Use deterministic policy services for source and registry checks
functional_requirements.requirement: Source classification, provenance capture, allowed-source policy, registry compatibility, budget enforcement, contract validation, and lifecycle transitions must be code-owned services rather than reasoning-agent lanes.
testable_consequences: Policy outcomes are reproducible and independently testable.
No routine Rights Analyst or manual rights queue appears in the certified workcell.

functional_requirements.title: Keep evaluation independent from production
functional_requirements.requirement: The Independent Visual Evaluator must receive the demand, candidate, composition render, relevant context, and profile without production-model self-approval or unnecessary producer reasoning history.
testable_consequences: Evaluator identity and profile are pinned in the acceptance receipt.
A candidate approved only by its producing model is ineligible.

functional_requirements.title: Make the Asset Commander a workflow authority, not a creative agent
functional_requirements.requirement: The Asset Commander controls state, routing, budgets, node validation, evaluation invocation, repair limits, promotion, and escalation but does not perform specialist creative analysis itself.
testable_consequences: Commander decisions are deterministic or based on typed specialist/evaluator contracts.
A general-agent Commander that silently combines all roles fails authority-separation tests.

functional_requirements.title: Register ComfyUI workflow profiles
functional_requirements.requirement: Every workflow profile must declare version, status, supported operations and asset families, required controls, compatible models and runtime profiles, geometry features, expected resources, benchmarks, known failures, and compiler adapter.
testable_consequences: The router can prove a workflow satisfies the plan’s capability requirements.
Ad hoc workflow JSON cannot run as a production profile.

functional_requirements.title: Register models, checkpoints, and VAEs
functional_requirements.requirement: Model records must pin architecture, hash, supported resolutions and operations, compatible text encoders/VAEs/workflows, VRAM, strengths, weaknesses, licensing/source policy class, benchmarks, and certified scope.
testable_consequences: Production binding fails when a required model relationship is incompatible.
A filename alone cannot identify a production model.

functional_requirements.title: Register LoRAs and adapters
functional_requirements.requirement: LoRA and adapter records must define base-model compatibility, intended capabilities, strength envelope, prohibited combinations, known failures, benchmark evidence, training provenance, and maturity.
testable_consequences: The compiler validates every stack and strength before execution.
An experimental or incompatible LoRA cannot silently enter a production run.

functional_requirements.title: Register conditioning and control profiles
functional_requirements.requirement: ControlNet, IP-Adapter, pose, depth, edge, mask, identity, regional prompting, camera, motion, and other controls must declare required inputs, applicable workflows, controllability, limits, and evaluator requirements.
testable_consequences: Plans can request capabilities independent of provider-specific node names.
A missing control dependency creates a capability blocker rather than best-effort improvisation.

functional_requirements.title: Register compute runtime profiles
functional_requirements.requirement: Runtime profiles must pin container digest, ComfyUI and Python/CUDA compatibility, custom-node lockfile, hardware class, model mounts, API, health checks, concurrency, timeouts, cache behavior, and certification.
testable_consequences: The scheduler chooses only profiles compatible with every bound capability.
A worker with an unregistered environment cannot receive production jobs.

functional_requirements.title: Maintain typed compatibility relationships
functional_requirements.requirement: The registry must model requires, compatible-with, conflicts-with, supersedes, validated-on, and prohibited-with relationships across workflows, models, VAEs, LoRAs, controls, nodes, evaluators, and runtimes.
testable_consequences: Registry validation catches broken or cyclic mandatory dependencies.
Compatibility cannot be inferred only from names or human memory.

functional_requirements.title: Promote capabilities through maturity states
functional_requirements.requirement: New or changed capabilities must move through experimental, benchmarked, shadow, limited-production, production, deprecated, and retired states with evidence and rollback.
testable_consequences: Production routing excludes capabilities below the requested policy level.
Registry edits cannot change an active run’s pinned bindings.

functional_requirements.title: Expose capability evidence and change impact
functional_requirements.requirement: The Control Tower and architecture tools must show benchmark results, cost and latency envelopes, known limitations, dependent plans/harnesses, and required regression suites for each capability.
testable_consequences: A proposed registry update calculates its blast radius before promotion.
A capability with unknown dependent certified paths cannot be removed.

functional_requirements.title: Compile every accepted demand into a Visual Production Plan
functional_requirements.requirement: Before provider execution, the service must create a schema-valid, versioned plan referencing the exact demand, objective, route, stages, inputs, outputs, capabilities, budgets, evaluations, fallbacks, and delivery requirements.
testable_consequences: Every execution node traces to one plan node and one authoritative need.
Direct demand-to-ComfyUI execution without a canonical plan is prohibited.

functional_requirements.title: Declare preservation and mutable bindings
functional_requirements.requirement: The plan must separate fields and properties that must be preserved from provider bindings, candidate variables, and repair variables that may change.
testable_consequences: Repair validation can prove no protected property was altered.
A binding not declared mutable cannot be changed by generation or repair.

functional_requirements.title: Model typed stages and dependencies
functional_requirements.requirement: Plan stages must declare actor/executor class, dependencies, contracts, checkpoints, expected artifacts, validators, invalidation, and eligible parallelism.
testable_consequences: The plan graph validator identifies orphan outputs, unavailable capabilities, illegal cycles, and unsatisfied dependencies.
A stage lacking an output contract cannot unlock downstream work.

functional_requirements.title: Express capability requirements provider-neutrally
functional_requirements.requirement: Plans must request functions such as identity conditioning, pose control, regional conditioning, inpainting, transparent extraction, or temporal consistency rather than hard-code ComfyUI node IDs as product requirements.
testable_consequences: Capability resolution can bind a different certified provider without changing plan meaning.
Provider-specific terminology cannot become the sole canonical representation.

functional_requirements.title: Compile deterministic provider graphs
functional_requirements.requirement: A versioned compiler must bind approved workflows, models, LoRAs, controls, runtimes, parameters, inputs, and output paths into ComfyUI JSON or another provider payload.
testable_consequences: The provider artifact includes a source plan hash and compiler version.
Manual runtime graph editing invalidates the compiled identity unless captured as a new governed profile.

functional_requirements.title: Compile evaluation and repair subgraphs
functional_requirements.requirement: The plan compiler must generate the required deterministic validation, VLM evaluation, composition simulation, recurrence/continuity comparison, repair, and delivery nodes alongside production.
testable_consequences: The execution graph contains every acceptance hard gate before promotion.
A production graph that omits the applicable evaluator cannot be certified.

functional_requirements.title: Version and amend plans without mutating demands
functional_requirements.requirement: Internal route, workflow, model, control, or runtime changes permitted by the demand create a new plan version with reason and impact while preserving the demand version.
testable_consequences: Valid prior outputs are reused when dependency analysis allows.
Demand-authority changes cannot be disguised as plan revisions.

functional_requirements.title: Preserve plan-to-execution reproducibility
functional_requirements.requirement: The service must retain the plan, compiled graphs, compiler identities, bindings, input hashes, events, outputs, and receipts needed to reproduce or audit the run.
testable_consequences: A dry-run can validate plan capability and budget without GPU execution.
An accepted asset whose executed graph cannot be reconciled with its plan fails traceability.

functional_requirements.title: Execute typed production nodes
functional_requirements.requirement: Every runtime node must declare actor type, inputs, outputs, dependencies, validations, timeout, infrastructure retries, checkpoint behavior, invalidation, event types, and eligible runtime profiles.
testable_consequences: The scheduler refuses incomplete node definitions.
An opaque long-running job without typed stages is outside certified scope.

functional_requirements.title: Persist an append-only production event stream
functional_requirements.requirement: The runtime must emit ordered typed events for plan compilation, queueing, node start/completion/failure, candidate creation, evaluation, repair, promotion, packaging, cancellation, and exceptions.
testable_consequences: Run state can be reconstructed from authoritative events plus immutable artifacts.
A UI-only status change without an event is invalid.

functional_requirements.title: Checkpoint successful nodes
functional_requirements.requirement: Committed node inputs, outputs, bindings, validation receipts, side effects, and dependency versions must be checkpointed for resume and targeted repair.
testable_consequences: An interrupted run resumes from the first invalid or incomplete node.
Successful retrieval, control preparation, or deterministic transforms are not repeated without invalidation.

functional_requirements.title: Separate infrastructure retries from quality repairs
functional_requirements.requirement: Container crashes, GPU loss, network timeout, cache corruption, or provider unavailability must use operational retry/fallback policies independent of the three VLM-directed quality rounds.
testable_consequences: Receipts report infrastructure attempts and quality rounds separately.
An infrastructure failure cannot consume or reset the quality-repair count.

functional_requirements.title: Enforce bounded timeouts, retries, and circuit breakers
functional_requirements.requirement: Each node/profile must declare timeout, retryable and non-retryable failures, maximum operational attempts, backoff, fallback, and circuit-breaker behavior.
testable_consequences: Exhaustion produces a typed terminal or exception state with preserved evidence.
The runtime cannot loop indefinitely on an unavailable provider or recurring error.

functional_requirements.title: Run dependency-safe bounded parallelism
functional_requirements.requirement: The scheduler may parallelize independent input preparation, candidate production, evaluation, or delivery work only within Budget Program concurrency and shared-resource constraints.
testable_consequences: Cancellation and failure of one branch do not corrupt valid siblings.
Parallel nodes with shared mutable state are rejected unless an explicit merge protocol exists.

functional_requirements.title: Support governed cancellation and backpressure
functional_requirements.requirement: Authorized callers may cancel non-promoted work; the service may communicate capacity, queue, and estimated-delay states without lowering quality gates.
testable_consequences: Cancellation preserves events, created artifacts, learning evidence, and cost receipts.
Backpressure cannot silently switch to uncertified or degraded production.

functional_requirements.title: Define explicit terminal states
functional_requirements.requirement: Runs must end in COMPLETED, INVALID_DEMAND, CAPABILITY_GAP, COST_APPROVAL_REQUIRED, HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED, DEPENDENCY_UNAVAILABLE, PRODUCTION_FAILED, CANCELLED, or another registered terminal state.
testable_consequences: Every terminal state has required evidence, owner, and allowed next action.
A stalled run cannot remain indefinitely in a nonterminal status without heartbeat or escalation.

functional_requirements.title: Store rich Visual Asset Memory records
functional_requirements.requirement: Accepted assets must be indexed by family/subtype, semantic roles, Activative functions, identities, environments, palette, expression, pose, geometry, production, evaluation, lineage, lifecycle, and certified uses.
testable_consequences: The memory record can answer both production and composition suitability questions.
A generic embedding-only asset record is insufficient.

functional_requirements.title: Create one Visual Usage Receipt per rendered use
functional_requirements.requirement: Every composition use must record asset version, harness, category, format profile, scene/slide, sequence position, syntactic role, composition function, BBOX/layer, neighboring elements, Activative function, transformation, recurrence intent, and rendered-context reference.
testable_consequences: The exact rendered use is inspectable independently of the source asset.
Usage count without context cannot drive fatigue decisions.

functional_requirements.title: Generate syntax-context fingerprints
functional_requirements.requirement: The system must derive comparable representations from the Visual Syntax Parse, composition geometry, neighboring relationships, recurrence intent, and rendered appearance for each use.
testable_consequences: Retrieval and recurrence evaluation can compare different files that express the same visual pattern.
File identity alone cannot represent contextual similarity.

functional_requirements.title: Classify recurrence contextually with a VLM
functional_requirements.requirement: Recurrence must be labeled beneficial recurrence, neutral reuse, productive variation, redundant repetition, fatiguing pattern, or contradictory recurrence using rendered context and sequence function.
testable_consequences: The VLM provides evidence and comparison references for its verdict.
Previous usage frequency cannot automatically reject a candidate.

functional_requirements.title: Retrieve assets through constraint-aware suitability
functional_requirements.requirement: Reuse ranking must combine semantic, Activative, composition, geometry, identity/continuity, syntax-role, transformation feasibility, quality, lifecycle, and recurrence signals.
testable_consequences: The result explains inclusion, exclusions, recommended route, and any transformation required.
High visual similarity cannot compensate for wrong role or continuity.

functional_requirements.title: Prioritize valid reuse before new production
functional_requirements.requirement: The Resolution Strategy Composer must evaluate exact reuse, deterministic variant, transformation, and composite reuse before new acquisition or generation when they can meet the demand.
testable_consequences: The plan records why reuse passed or failed.
New generation without a memory query is nonconformant for asset families with memory enabled.

functional_requirements.title: Preserve continuity without creating false fatigue
functional_requirements.requirement: Character identity, visual world, persistent format instruments, and deliberate motifs may repeat when the current syntax context and sequence purpose justify recurrence.
testable_consequences: Continuity-positive recurrence can be exempted from raw-frequency penalties through explicit evidence.
A model that penalizes required character identity repetition fails recurrence benchmarks.

functional_requirements.title: Invalidate or warn on superseded memory
functional_requirements.requirement: Superseded, regressed, deprecated, or contextually unsafe assets and recipes must be excluded or penalized according to lifecycle policy, and affected in-progress uses must revalidate.
testable_consequences: Historical use remains visible with the version that was actually consumed.
A superseded asset cannot be returned as current production-ready without an explicit compatibility exception.

functional_requirements.title: Support a hybrid compute pool
functional_requirements.requirement: The scheduler must support certified local workstation GPUs, self-hosted GPU servers, cloud GPU instances, autoscaled temporary workers, and approved external providers through common job contracts.
testable_consequences: The plan can route according to capability, policy, queue, cost, and availability.
No single permanent ComfyUI server is required as a product assumption.

functional_requirements.title: Use immutable container runtime profiles
functional_requirements.requirement: Self-hosted jobs must run from digest-pinned OCI images containing approved ComfyUI, Python/CUDA, custom-node lockfile, compiler adapter, API executor, health probes, and telemetry agent.
testable_consequences: Execution receipts identify the exact image and environment.
Mutable in-place package installation on a production worker invalidates certification.

functional_requirements.title: Manage models and LoRAs as versioned mounted resources
functional_requirements.requirement: Weights must be hash-verified, registered, mounted or cached separately from container images, and associated with storage, compatibility, and eviction policy.
testable_consequences: The scheduler confirms every required weight is available or transferable before job start.
Unverified model files cannot be loaded by production workers.

functional_requirements.title: Expose a uniform asynchronous worker API
functional_requirements.requirement: Certified worker adapters must support submit, status, heartbeat/progress, cancel, result retrieval, logs/receipts, and failure classification.
testable_consequences: The runtime can replace one compatible worker without changing the canonical plan.
A worker requiring manual desktop interaction is outside autonomous production scope.

functional_requirements.title: Route by capability and expected value
functional_requirements.requirement: Scheduling must consider workflow/model/control requirements, VRAM, GPU class, queue delay, warm model cache, data locality, expected runtime, cost, privacy policy, maturity, and fallback.
testable_consequences: The selected runtime and alternatives are preserved in the plan receipt.
Fastest-available routing cannot bypass compatibility or certification.

functional_requirements.title: Enforce worker and job isolation
functional_requirements.requirement: Jobs must not mutate canonical workflows, model weights, accepted assets, registry state, other jobs, or authoritative demands; experimental custom nodes run in isolated profiles.
testable_consequences: Fault-injection tests prove cross-job and registry containment.
A compromised or failed worker can be quarantined without corrupting stored artifacts.

functional_requirements.title: Recover from worker and provider failure
functional_requirements.requirement: The fabric must detect missed heartbeats, container crashes, unavailable GPUs, corrupted caches, API timeouts, and partial uploads, then retry or fail over according to profile policy.
testable_consequences: Recovery resumes from committed workflow checkpoints and preserves quality-round counts.
The operator is not required to restart routine failed jobs.

functional_requirements.title: Prove local and cloud reference execution
functional_requirements.requirement: Implementation authorization requires one self-hosted/local and one cloud container profile to execute representative Format 02 workflows with equivalent contracts, receipts, recovery, and benchmark behavior.
testable_consequences: The proof pins hardware, image, nodes, weights, workflow, and results.
Architecture cannot claim a hybrid fabric based only on design documents.

functional_requirements.title: Detect reusable capability gaps
functional_requirements.requirement: A gap may be proposed only when representative demands or repeated repairs show an unmet recurring identity, character, environment, visual-language, pose, motion, control, workflow, or evaluator capability.
testable_consequences: The proposal includes failed certified alternatives, expected reuse, and affected syntax contexts.
One unattractive candidate or ordinary stochastic failure cannot trigger training.

functional_requirements.title: Require evidence-sufficiency analysis
functional_requirements.requirement: Before training or adaptation, the system must assess reference count, diversity, quality, identity consistency, captions, rights/source policy class, duplicates, exclusions, and coverage of intended conditions.
testable_consequences: Insufficient evidence produces a typed blocker and collection plan.
Training cannot begin from an undocumented or contradictory dataset.

functional_requirements.title: Compile a typed Capability Development Plan
functional_requirements.requirement: The plan must declare capability goal, target base, dataset contract, preprocessing, training method, hyperparameter search, compute budget, baseline, evaluation profiles, promotion path, rollback, and prohibited use.
testable_consequences: Every training job traces to the approved plan.
Ad hoc notebook training cannot create a registry-eligible production capability.

functional_requirements.title: Prepare datasets in isolated, reproducible pipelines
functional_requirements.requirement: Dataset normalization, deduplication, captioning, cropping, quality inspection, train/validation splits, and manifests must execute in versioned sandboxes with immutable source references.
testable_consequences: The exact training dataset can be reconstructed and diffed.
Untracked manual dataset edits invalidate the training receipt.

functional_requirements.title: Benchmark against relevant controls
functional_requirements.requirement: New LoRAs, adapters, controls, workflows, or model adaptations must be compared with base model, current registered controls, existing stacks, and other credible alternatives on representative demands and syntax contexts.
testable_consequences: The benchmark measures intended improvement, responsiveness, diversity, overfitting, contamination, composition control, cost, latency, and regressions.
A capability cannot be promoted solely from showcase outputs.

functional_requirements.title: Detect contamination and compatibility regressions
functional_requirements.requirement: Evaluation must test identity drift, style leakage, prompt insensitivity, forbidden combinations, base-model incompatibility, unwanted memorization, composition degradation, and failure outside declared applicability.
testable_consequences: Critical regression blocks promotion and is linked to evidence.
A capability that improves one target while breaking certified shared behavior cannot enter production silently.

functional_requirements.title: Use staged registry promotion
functional_requirements.requirement: Successful capability candidates must move through experimental, benchmarked, shadow, limited-production, and production states with pinned versions and approved applicability.
testable_consequences: Shadow decisions are compared against the current baseline without controlling accepted assets.
An experimental LoRA cannot be selected under production-only policy.

functional_requirements.title: Preserve rollback and deprecation
functional_requirements.requirement: Every promoted capability must retain its predecessor, migration/compatibility notes, dependent paths, rollback procedure, and retirement criteria.
testable_consequences: A production regression can revert routing to the preceding certified version without losing run reproducibility.
Deleting or replacing the only copy of a capability used by historical assets is prohibited.

functional_requirements.title: Register asset-family-aware evaluation profiles
functional_requirements.requirement: Each certified asset family, subtype, route, and composition context must map to an evaluation profile declaring deterministic validators, VLM programs, required contexts, dimensions, thresholds, hard gates, failure codes, repair mappings, and applicability.
testable_consequences: The plan compiler selects the exact profile before candidate production.
A universal unversioned evaluator cannot approve every asset class.

functional_requirements.title: Run deterministic technical validation first
functional_requirements.requirement: Code-owned validators must check file integrity, dimensions, aspect ratio, duration/frame rate, codec, alpha, masks, blank/corrupt output, metadata, receipt completeness, and budget before semantic evaluation.
testable_consequences: Technical failures are localized without spending VLM evaluation unnecessarily.
A candidate failing a technical hard gate cannot be promoted by VLM preference.

functional_requirements.title: Run independent asset-level VLM evaluation
functional_requirements.requirement: The evaluator must compare the isolated candidate with required subject, action, expression, pose, gesture, gaze, identity, environment, visual properties, semantic intent, Activative function, wrong-reading locks, and artifact quality.
testable_consequences: Failures include evidence regions/time ranges and calibrated confidence.
The producing model’s self-description cannot substitute for independent inspection.

functional_requirements.title: Run composition-level VLM evaluation
functional_requirements.requirement: Applicable candidates must be rendered inside their intended BBOX, layers, text reservations, neighboring elements, sequence role, and feed/viewing profile before composition-effectiveness approval.
testable_consequences: Evaluation checks hierarchy, focal visibility, negative space, crop safety, gaze/motion direction, collision, and Activative function.
Standalone beauty or correctness cannot compensate for a failed composed use.

functional_requirements.title: Run syntax-aware recurrence and continuity evaluation
functional_requirements.requirement: The evaluator must compare the candidate’s proposed rendered use against relevant Visual Usage Receipts, syntax fingerprints, identities, environments, sequence roles, and recurrence intent.
testable_consequences: It classifies recurrence and explains whether continuity or progression is helped or harmed.
Raw usage frequency cannot be the sole fatigue signal.

functional_requirements.title: Run temporal evaluation where applicable
functional_requirements.requirement: Motion and video profiles must evaluate frame-to-frame identity, motion, gesture completion, camera continuity, flicker, artifacts, loop integrity, duration, and timing against sequence purpose.
testable_consequences: Temporal failures include time ranges and responsible production layers.
A passing first frame cannot approve a failing animation.

functional_requirements.title: Synthesize verdicts with hard-gate precedence
functional_requirements.requirement: The evaluation contract must preserve per-layer verdicts, dimensions, evidence, failure codes, repair owner, and confidence; acceptance requires all applicable hard gates.
testable_consequences: Weighted rankings are calculated only among eligible passing candidates.
High technical or aesthetic scores cannot hide a failed semantic, Activative, or composition gate.

functional_requirements.title: Certify evaluator versions independently
functional_requirements.requirement: VLM programs and threshold profiles must pass labeled accepted/rejected/borderline, recurrence, temporal, responsible-layer, and repair usefulness benchmarks before shadow and production promotion.
testable_consequences: Acceptance receipts pin evaluator model, program, profile, thresholds, and context hashes.
A newer model version cannot replace the production evaluator without regression evidence.

functional_requirements.title: Emit a typed repair contract for every quality rerun
functional_requirements.requirement: The evaluator must identify failure code, severity, evidence region/time range, responsible layer, preserved properties, permitted and prohibited changes, invalidated nodes, reusable outputs, expected correction evidence, and repair round.
testable_consequences: The runtime can validate the repair before applying it.
Free-form 'try again' instructions cannot trigger a quality rerun.

functional_requirements.title: Preserve successful properties explicitly
functional_requirements.requirement: Repair plans must freeze all accepted identities, expressions, geometry, palette, environment, text-safe regions, continuity, and semantic properties not responsible for the failure.
testable_consequences: Post-repair evaluation compares preserved properties with the parent candidate.
A repair that fixes one failure by drifting a preserved property is rejected.

functional_requirements.title: Apply the least disruptive repair hierarchy
functional_requirements.requirement: The system must prefer deterministic correction, then parameter correction, conditioning correction, workflow/capability substitution, and finally approved resolution-strategy amendment according to the failure and plan.
testable_consequences: The selected level and rejected lower levels are receipted.
A full regeneration cannot be the default when a deterministic or local repair can resolve the issue.

functional_requirements.title: Require causal production changes
functional_requirements.requirement: Every quality rerun must modify at least one binding or execution route plausibly responsible for the diagnosed failure, except evaluator-authorized stochastic seed exploration.
testable_consequences: The repair receipt records old and new values and causal hypothesis.
Repeated identical bindings with only an unexplained new seed fail the blind-retry guard.

functional_requirements.title: Rerun only invalidated nodes
functional_requirements.requirement: The Repair and Invalidation Graph must preserve valid references, masks, controls, background plates, runtime bindings, and deterministic transformations while rescheduling affected production and evaluation nodes.
testable_consequences: Repair cost and time reflect the reduced graph region.
Unrelated expensive stages cannot be replayed without dependency invalidation.

functional_requirements.title: Enforce three quality-repair rounds
functional_requirements.requirement: The system may perform at most three VLM-directed quality rounds: local correction, strengthened/substituted controls, and approved fallback route or strategy.
testable_consequences: Round exhaustion emits HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED or CAPABILITY_GAP with the best evidence and attempts.
A fourth autonomous quality round is constitutionally blocked.

functional_requirements.title: Separate repair from demand amendment
functional_requirements.requirement: Repairs may use only mutable plan bindings and internal production alternatives authorized by the accepted demand; semantic or out-of-tolerance changes must become amendment proposals.
testable_consequences: The repair validator rejects prohibited field changes.
An evaluator cannot lower a quality threshold to declare its repair successful.

functional_requirements.title: Capture repair effectiveness for steering and regression
functional_requirements.requirement: Every repair must record failure context, syntax role, changed bindings, cost, latency, outcome, preserved-property regressions, and candidate/evaluator versions for later Steering Recipe and registry analysis.
testable_consequences: Repeated failure patterns can trigger routing warnings or capability-gap proposals.
Learning evidence cannot directly mutate production defaults.

functional_requirements.title: Register six Budget Programs
functional_requirements.requirement: The product must define Lean, Standard, Premium, Exploration, Capability Learning, and Custom programs with candidate, parallelism, workflow diversity, evaluator, repair, time, cost, and learning semantics.
testable_consequences: Each run pins one program version in its plan and receipt.
A raw GPU setting without program semantics cannot be selected as the primary policy.

functional_requirements.title: Expose program selection in the supervisory menu
functional_requirements.requirement: Authorized operators and callers must be able to select an eligible Budget Program and see expected cost, latency, candidate count, evaluator depth, experimental scope, and learning outputs before execution.
testable_consequences: The UI distinguishes production and learning budgets.
High-cost or experimental programs require the caller authority defined by policy.

functional_requirements.title: Compile bounded candidate portfolios
functional_requirements.requirement: Each plan must declare initial and maximum candidates, maximum parallel jobs, exploration dimensions, fixed properties, candidate classes, selection policy, and budget ceilings.
testable_consequences: The scheduler cannot exceed portfolio or Budget Program limits.
Candidate generation without declared purpose or limit is prohibited.

functional_requirements.title: Use controlled variation for exploration
functional_requirements.requirement: Exploration and Capability Learning portfolios must vary declared factors while preserving comparable baselines, fixed variables, hypothesis, and measurement plan.
testable_consequences: The system can attribute quality differences to tested interventions.
Indiscriminate random prompt/seed sweeps do not qualify as learning evidence.

functional_requirements.title: Filter hard-gate failures before ranking
functional_requirements.requirement: Every candidate must pass technical, semantic, Activative, composition, and applicable continuity/temporal hard gates before quality/cost/latency ranking.
testable_consequences: Ineligible candidates remain available for diagnosis but cannot win.
The first completed candidate or highest aesthetic score cannot override failed gates.

functional_requirements.title: Select the best validated candidate within budget
functional_requirements.requirement: Among eligible candidates, the selector must use the registered ranking profile across fidelity, effectiveness, continuity, distinctiveness, technical quality, editability, repair risk, cost, and latency.
testable_consequences: The selected candidate and credible alternatives are receipted.
Completion speed is only a declared constraint or tie-breaker, never the sole winner criterion.

functional_requirements.title: Support adaptive expansion and early stopping
functional_requirements.requirement: The portfolio may expand when evidence shows valuable unresolved uncertainty and may stop when a passing candidate exceeds high-confidence thresholds and further expected value is low.
testable_consequences: Expansion and early stop decisions include evaluator evidence and remaining budget.
The system cannot exhaust a high budget automatically when a strong accepted asset already exists.

functional_requirements.title: Emit complete budget and portfolio receipts
functional_requirements.requirement: Each run must report estimated versus actual cost/time, GPU seconds, candidates created/evaluated/passing, parallelism, repairs, early stopping, selected asset, and learning artifacts.
testable_consequences: Operators can compare quality gain per additional cost across programs.
Hidden candidate or evaluator cost fails cost-governance validation.

functional_requirements.title: Capture steering evidence from production
functional_requirements.requirement: Candidate portfolios, evaluations, repairs, syntax contexts, bindings, costs, and final outcomes must produce structured evidence suitable for recurring pattern analysis.
testable_consequences: Evidence distinguishes correlation, controlled comparison, and validated intervention.
Raw winning prompts cannot be promoted directly as production knowledge.

functional_requirements.title: Compile versioned Visual Steering Recipes
functional_requirements.requirement: A Steering Recipe must define applicability, failure/success pattern, compatible workflows and assets, intervention, preserved properties, evidence, observed runs, control comparison, regression cases, cost effect, lifecycle, and authority.
testable_consequences: Recipes are independently inspectable and benchmarkable.
A recipe without applicability boundaries cannot enter shadow routing.

functional_requirements.title: Promote recipes through governed learning states
functional_requirements.requirement: Recipes must move through proposed, experimental, validated, shadow, limited-production, production, deprecated, and retired states with minimum evidence and rollback.
testable_consequences: Production routing uses only policy-eligible recipe states.
A single successful run cannot alter defaults.

functional_requirements.title: Publish durable knowledge through CMF-OKF
functional_requirements.requirement: The product must project selected workflows, models, LoRAs, failure patterns, repair patterns, Steering Recipes, syntax usage contexts, benchmarks, and operator knowledge into OKF-compatible Markdown with CMF frontmatter extensions.
testable_consequences: Concepts remain readable, version-controllable, portable, and linked to canonical records.
OKF documents cannot become the authoritative lifecycle or execution store.

functional_requirements.title: Define typed CMF knowledge relationships
functional_requirements.requirement: CMF-OKF concepts and the retrieval graph must support explicit edges such as derived-from, validated-by, compatible-with, contradicts, supersedes, repairs, failed-under, applies-to, observed-in, consumed-by, and shares-syntax-with.
testable_consequences: Graph traversal can distinguish evidence, compatibility, contradiction, and succession.
Untyped prose links alone cannot govern production retrieval.

functional_requirements.title: Apply authority and compatibility filters before similarity search
functional_requirements.requirement: Retrieval must first filter by lifecycle, authority, category, format, asset family, syntax role, Activative function, failure code, workflow/model compatibility, validity, and current demand constraints.
testable_consequences: Incompatible or superseded concepts are excluded or marked explicitly.
Semantic similarity cannot override hard eligibility.

functional_requirements.title: Use hybrid multimodal retrieval and VLM reranking
functional_requirements.requirement: Eligible memory must be ranked using lexical search, text embeddings, image embeddings, composition embeddings, syntax fingerprints, graph proximity, evidence quality, and VLM comparison with current demand, composition, failure, and preservation constraints.
testable_consequences: Results include scores, reasons, conflicts, and exclusions.
The system cannot rely solely on one vector index.

functional_requirements.title: Compile Minimum Complete Context with contradiction coverage
functional_requirements.requirement: The JIT retrieval compiler must select only relevant frontmatter, body sections, typed contract references, exceptions, superseding knowledge, failure cases, and material conflicting evidence needed by the current node.
testable_consequences: The compilation receipt records included, excluded, compressed, and unavailable resources.
The model may not receive the entire memory corpus by default.

functional_requirements.title: Render a live Visual Asset Demand overview
functional_requirements.requirement: The Control Tower must show caller, demand version, harness/category/profile, asset role, Activative function, composition intent, selected Budget Program, current state, blockers, expected cost/latency, and next authorized action.
testable_consequences: Every displayed value links to its authoritative contract or event.
The dashboard cannot infer or edit demand authority from UI-only state.

functional_requirements.title: Visualize the Visual Production Plan and node graph
functional_requirements.requirement: Operators must inspect stages, dependencies, actor/executor, capabilities, bindings, status, checkpoints, invalidation, retries, runtime placement, and events.
testable_consequences: The graph distinguishes infrastructure and quality paths.
An opaque 'generating' spinner is insufficient for production observability.

functional_requirements.title: Compare candidates and evaluation evidence
functional_requirements.requirement: The console must support side-by-side candidate, composition render, score, hard-gate, evidence region/time range, failure code, recurrence, cost, and repair comparison.
testable_consequences: The selected asset and rejected alternatives retain reasons.
The operator is not required to manually approve passing routine candidates.

functional_requirements.title: Expose asset lineage and usage context
functional_requirements.requirement: Operators must navigate from accepted assets to references, candidates, repairs, master, delivery variants, geometry, prior and current syntax contexts, usage receipts, recurrence verdicts, and supersession.
testable_consequences: Published and in-progress consumers are distinguishable.
A lineage view may not omit rejected or repaired branches needed for audit.

functional_requirements.title: Expose compute, queue, and worker health
functional_requirements.requirement: The console must show queued/running jobs, runtime profile, GPU class, model cache, worker heartbeat, timeout, retries, failover, cost, and capacity conditions.
testable_consequences: Operational failures can be diagnosed without logging into the ComfyUI worker desktop.
Secrets and unrestricted worker shell access are not exposed through standard views.

functional_requirements.title: Provide policy-first operator controls
functional_requirements.requirement: Authorized users must select Budget Program, production priority, experimental policy, cost/time ceilings, post-failure behavior, cancellation, and capability-development authorization through governed controls.
testable_consequences: Actions are validated against caller scope and generate receipts.
Routine controls do not include manual seed, prompt, LoRA-strength, or node editing.

functional_requirements.title: Present typed human-exception packages
functional_requirements.requirement: After authorized escalation, the UI must show trigger, best candidate, passing and failing dimensions, attempted repairs, cost, preserved state, recommended choices, consequences, and downstream authority owner.
testable_consequences: The chosen response creates a typed event or new demand/plan version.
An operator cannot silently override a constitutional hard gate from a generic approve button.

functional_requirements.title: Provide cross-run quality and operations analytics
functional_requirements.requirement: The Control Tower must report completion, first-pass acceptance, repair rounds, human exceptions, failure codes, recurrence judgments, route/workflow/model performance, cost, latency, capability gaps, and benchmark drift by asset family and syntax context.
testable_consequences: Metrics can drive governed learning proposals and regression investigation.
Analytics cannot update production registry policy without the promotion process.

functional_requirements.title: Expose an asynchronous submission API
functional_requirements.requirement: The service must accept an authorized demand reference, execution policy, callbacks or event subscription, and idempotency key, then return a durable execution ID and submission receipt without holding the connection for GPU completion.
testable_consequences: Long-running candidate, repair, and learning runs remain observable after submission.
A synchronous request-response-only interface is insufficient.

functional_requirements.title: Publish versioned execution status
functional_requirements.requirement: The service must expose registered states from ACCEPTED through validation, planning, capability resolution, queue, production, evaluation, repair, promotion, packaging, and completion plus typed terminal exceptions.
testable_consequences: Callers can poll or subscribe without controlling internal nodes.
Unregistered ambiguous states cannot appear in the public contract.

functional_requirements.title: Emit versioned Visual Asset Events
functional_requirements.requirement: Events must identify event/run/demand versions, state, candidate/round when applicable, reason/failure, references, timestamps, and compatibility version.
testable_consequences: Consumers can rebuild relevant delegation state from events.
Internal log prose cannot serve as the only integration event.

functional_requirements.title: Return a provenance-complete Asset Result Contract
functional_requirements.requirement: Completed results must list accepted assets and variants, geometry, evaluations, production, budget, syntax-context, authorization, limitations, and downstream action.
testable_consequences: Downstream composition can validate every referenced artifact and hash.
A raw image URL alone is not a completed service response.

functional_requirements.title: Support authorized cancellation
functional_requirements.requirement: Registered callers may cancel non-promoted work according to state and policy; cancellation preserves events, artifacts, compute receipts, and reusable learning evidence.
testable_consequences: The final cancellation receipt identifies work stopped and work retained.
Cancellation cannot delete accepted historical assets or canonical evidence.

functional_requirements.title: Communicate backpressure without quality degradation
functional_requirements.requirement: The service may return queue depth, capacity, expected delay, runtime alternatives, and scheduling policy while preserving quality thresholds and demand authority.
testable_consequences: Callers can choose to wait, cancel, or authorize a compatible cost/latency change.
Load cannot silently route to uncertified capabilities or reduce candidate/evaluator gates.

functional_requirements.title: Enforce delegation authorization
functional_requirements.requirement: Only authorized callers may submit demands, select high-cost or experimental programs, cancel runs, authorize amendments, accept degraded results, or request capability development.
testable_consequences: All public actions are scoped and receipted.
An internal service identity cannot impersonate the owning Content Harness for semantic amendments.

functional_requirements.title: Prepare the separate Delegation PRD handoff
functional_requirements.requirement: This PRD must enumerate required shared contracts, responsibility boundaries, versioning, compatibility, amendment flow, error taxonomy, and test fixtures while explicitly deferring final shared schema authority to the Content Harness ↔ Visual Asset Editor Delegation PRD.
testable_consequences: Architecture can proceed with representative fixtures but not silently freeze cross-product contracts as editor-only property.
Any shared-boundary conflict is recorded for the Delegation planning session.

functional_requirements.title: Detect and classify blocking demand conflicts
functional_requirements.requirement: The system must identify contradictory semantic, composition, continuity, control, capability, budget, timing, or delivery constraints that remain infeasible after authorized routes and repairs.
testable_consequences: The conflict contract identifies code, severity, evidence, attempts, and responsible authority.
The editor cannot hide a conflict by producing the closest-looking result.

functional_requirements.title: Preserve the accepted demand during conflict handling
functional_requirements.requirement: Conflict analysis and amendment options must reference but never mutate the accepted demand version.
testable_consequences: The Control Tower shows the unchanged demand and all proposed changes separately.
An amendment proposal cannot become effective through plan or UI state alone.

functional_requirements.title: Allow internal production amendments within demand authority
functional_requirements.requirement: The editor may version its plan to change approved workflow, model, LoRA, controls, runtime, candidate strategy, or permitted route when all demand constraints remain satisfied.
testable_consequences: The plan amendment states reason, invalidation, budget impact, and preserved authority.
Internal amendments cannot relax semantic or out-of-tolerance composition requirements.

functional_requirements.title: Return demand-level amendment proposals to the owner
functional_requirements.requirement: Changes to subject, visible action, Activative function, sequence role, identity, continuity, wrong-reading locks, protected geometry outside tolerance, or quality threshold must be proposed to the owning Content Harness.
testable_consequences: Each option describes expected feasibility, semantic/Activative impact, cost, and evidence.
The editor cannot approve or select the owner’s demand amendment.

functional_requirements.title: Route constitutional amendments upstream
functional_requirements.requirement: Proposals that alter asset ontology, category grammar, authority boundaries, production hard gates, Builder architecture, or canonical shared doctrine must enter the validated Builder governance process.
testable_consequences: The editor provides evidence without modifying upstream constitutions.
A local release cannot redefine a category or Builder law.

functional_requirements.title: Create a new demand version for accepted amendments
functional_requirements.requirement: When the owner accepts an option, the caller must submit a new Visual Asset Demand version with supersession, amendment source, reason, preserved authority, and changed fields.
testable_consequences: Dependency analysis determines which prior work can be reused.
The original failed demand and conflict remain immutable.

functional_requirements.title: Govern degraded acceptance
functional_requirements.requirement: A below-threshold result may be accepted only through a new demand version or owner-authorized exception identifying the relaxed requirement, risk, limitation, and downstream visibility, and only when no constitutional hard gate is violated.
testable_consequences: The Asset Result Contract records degradation explicitly.
A standard operator approval cannot bypass meaning-related gates.

functional_requirements.title: Benchmark conflict and amendment behavior
functional_requirements.requirement: The portfolio must include impossible geometry, contradictory identity/continuity, unsupported capability, inadequate budget, and semantic-relaxation cases to verify correct conflict classification and authority routing.
testable_consequences: The system is scored on refusing silent substitution and proposing useful bounded options.
A model that always rejects or always compromises fails the benchmark.

functional_requirements.title: Maintain a layered visual benchmark corpus
functional_requirements.requirement: The portfolio must contain representative real demands, behavior goldens, known failures, adversarial cases, controlled mutations, incomplete/conflicting demands, recurrence contexts, repair cases, capability-transfer cases, and out-of-distribution cases.
testable_consequences: Each case declares expected behavior, hard gates, scoring, and protected labels.
Showcase assets alone cannot certify the system.
