functional_requirements.title: Score independent dimensions with hard gates
functional_requirements.requirement: Certification must report semantic fidelity, Activative fidelity, composition effectiveness, syntax conformance, identity/continuity, technical validity, temporal stability where applicable, provenance/reproducibility, repair precision, evaluator accuracy, cost, latency, and intervention.
testable_consequences: A failed constitutional dimension blocks promotion regardless of average.
One composite score cannot replace the full scorecard.

functional_requirements.title: Benchmark the VLM evaluator itself
functional_requirements.requirement: The evaluator set must include accepted, rejected, borderline, identity drift, wrong action, composition failure, beneficial recurrence, fatiguing recurrence, temporal failure, repairable and nonrepairable cases.
testable_consequences: Metrics include hard-failure recall, false rejection, failure-code and responsible-layer accuracy, recurrence judgment, repair usefulness, and confidence calibration.
An evaluator cannot certify its own benchmark labels.

functional_requirements.title: Test repair precision and preservation
functional_requirements.requirement: Repair benchmarks must verify root-cause layer, allowed binding changes, frozen properties, selective invalidation, improvement, regression absence, and three-round stop behavior.
testable_consequences: Before/after assets and receipts are retained.
A repair process that routinely redoes the whole asset or drifts identity cannot pass.

functional_requirements.title: Test compute and workflow recovery
functional_requirements.requirement: Certification must inject container crash, GPU loss, API timeout, model-cache corruption, missing node, queue interruption, worker replacement, checkpoint restore, and provider fallback failures.
testable_consequences: State, lineage, and quality-round counts remain correct after recovery.
Manual worker intervention cannot be required for the certified routine path.

functional_requirements.title: Use staged capability and product promotion
functional_requirements.requirement: Workflows, models, LoRAs, evaluators, Steering Recipes, runtime profiles, and product releases must move through experimental, benchmarked, shadow, limited-production, and certified-production stages with rollback.
testable_consequences: The release receipt identifies authorized scope and unresolved limitations.
Passing unit tests alone cannot authorize production.

functional_requirements.title: Prove the complete Format 02 vertical slice
functional_requirements.requirement: Release 1 must process real Minimal Coach Theatre demands for character identity, pose, expression, gesture, gaze, environment, composition, continuity, syntax role, Activative function, wrong-reading locks, candidates, repair, geometry, result, and downstream composition consumption without routine manual work.
testable_consequences: The reference slice runs on one local/self-hosted and one cloud Docker GPU profile.
A good standalone image that is not consumed and evaluated in the Format 02 composition does not complete the slice.

functional_requirements.title: Represent broader scope honestly
functional_requirements.requirement: All eight asset families may have schemas, registries, interfaces, and benchmark placeholders, but only capabilities that pass their required portfolio and release receipt may be routed as production-certified.
testable_consequences: UI and compatibility manifests expose limited and uncertified scope.
Release 1 cannot imply full video, documentary retrieval, advanced diagrams, UI reconstruction, or other uncertified production support.

functional_requirements.title: Preserve the validated Atomic Harness Builder architecture
functional_requirements.requirement: The product must treat the Builder lifecycle, Harness IR, Capability Ownership Map, Phase/Context/Contract graphs, JIT Skill architecture, Workflow Runtime, Control Tower, Repair doctrine, Development Capsule, category constitutions, and Content Harness semantic authority as frozen upstream contracts.
testable_consequences: The preservation contract maps each editor feature to compatible upstream mechanisms.
An editor implementation cannot redesign these systems without a governed upstream amendment.

functional_requirements.title: Version product and internal capability layers independently
functional_requirements.requirement: The editor product, public contracts, Visual Production Plan IR, capability registries, evaluation profiles, compute profiles, CMF-OKF profile, and category certifications must each declare versions and compatibility.
testable_consequences: Active runs pin exact versions and hashes.
A registry update cannot silently alter an in-flight run.

functional_requirements.title: Publish a release compatibility manifest
functional_requirements.requirement: Every release must declare supported Builder target profiles, delegation contract versions, plan IR, category/format profiles, registries, runtimes, certified scope, migration need, and rollback support.
testable_consequences: Compatibility tests cover every claimed combination.
Undeclared compatibility cannot be inferred from successful manual use.

functional_requirements.title: Classify patch, minor, and major change behavior
functional_requirements.requirement: Patch changes fix implementation without contract behavior change; minor changes add backward-compatible optional behavior; major changes alter mandatory contracts, lifecycle, authority, hard gates, or canonical IR and require migration.
testable_consequences: Release tooling validates the declared class against actual schema and behavior changes.
Breaking changes cannot ship under patch or minor version.

functional_requirements.title: Run representative compatibility and rollback tests
functional_requirements.requirement: Before release, the system must execute current and declared older demand fixtures, Format 02 reference cases, result parsing, geometry, events/exceptions, registry bindings, and rollback to the preceding certified release.
testable_consequences: Results appear in the release receipt.
A release with an untested claimed compatibility path cannot be certified.

functional_requirements.title: Enforce implementation readiness states
functional_requirements.requirement: The lifecycle must distinguish PRD_DRAFT, PRD_APPROVED, ARCHITECTURE_IN_PROGRESS, ARCHITECTURE_VALIDATED, CONTRACT_FIXTURES_READY, REFERENCE_SLICE_READY, IMPLEMENTATION_AUTHORIZED, LIMITED_PRODUCTION_CERTIFIED, and PRODUCTION_CERTIFIED.
testable_consequences: Each state has typed evidence and transition gates.
PRD_APPROVED cannot be interpreted as implementation authorization.

functional_requirements.title: Generate a traceable Visual Asset Editor Development Capsule
functional_requirements.requirement: Implementation authorization must package approved PRD, decisions, requirements, Architecture, preservation contract, public/internal schemas, representative fixtures, Format 02 reference slice, benchmarks, compatibility, epics/stories, feature specs, and readiness receipt with source traceability.
testable_consequences: Every generated implementation artifact maps to requirements and architecture nodes.
A folder of ungoverned scaffolding does not satisfy the capsule.

functional_requirements.title: Enforce binding implementation prohibitions
functional_requirements.requirement: Readiness must block monolithic general-agent workflows, producer self-approval, blind retries, more than three quality rounds, frequency-only fatigue, OKF as operational truth, uncontrolled high-budget generation, one-failure LoRA training, silent demand relaxation, uncertified scope claims, routine manual ComfyUI operation, or upstream architecture mutation.
testable_consequences: The readiness report names each prohibition and evidence of compliance.
Any known violation forces a blocked authorization result.

non_functional_requirements.title: Preserve demand authority
non_functional_requirements.requirement: The system must not change authorized semantic intent, Activative function, sequence role, composition role, identity, continuity, or wrong-reading constraints outside a new approved demand version.

non_functional_requirements.title: Separate observation from inference
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Visual observations, derived geometry, VLM interpretations, generated proposals, and human-authorized decisions must remain explicitly typed and traceable.

non_functional_requirements.title: Hard-gate meaning failures
non_functional_requirements.requirement: A technically attractive asset with failed semantic or Activative fidelity must be ineligible for acceptance.

non_functional_requirements.title: Preserve category and format context
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Evaluation, memory, and routing must retain the requesting category, format profile, atomic harness, and Visual Syntax role.

non_functional_requirements.title: Prevent silent demand degradation
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Budget, queue pressure, provider failure, or workflow fallback must not lower semantic or composition quality thresholds without a typed amendment.

non_functional_requirements.title: Idempotent submissions
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Identical demand versions and idempotency keys must not create duplicate production runs.

non_functional_requirements.title: Resumable workflows
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Committed nodes must resume from checkpoints after interruption without replaying valid work.

non_functional_requirements.title: Bounded quality repair
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Quality repair must stop after three rounds and transition to a typed terminal or exception state.

non_functional_requirements.title: Failure containment
non_functional_requirements.requirement: A node or worker failure must not mutate unrelated jobs, accepted assets, canonical registries, or authoritative contracts.

non_functional_requirements.title: Rollback readiness
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Certified releases and capability versions must support rollback with preserved asset and execution lineage.

non_functional_requirements.title: Budget-class latency targets
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Each Budget Program must define expected queue, production, evaluation, and total wall-clock envelopes.

non_functional_requirements.title: Node-level timing
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Every production and evaluation node must emit start, completion, duration, queue, and wait telemetry.

non_functional_requirements.title: Efficient checkpoint reuse
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Repairs and resumes must reuse non-invalidated deterministic work, references, controls, and accepted intermediate assets.

non_functional_requirements.title: Adaptive early stopping
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Candidate exploration may stop when a passing candidate exceeds configured confidence and expected additional value is low.

non_functional_requirements.title: No hidden latency degradation
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Model, workflow, container, or evaluator updates must be benchmarked for p50 and p95 latency changes before promotion.

non_functional_requirements.title: Pre-execution estimate
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Every production plan must estimate GPU time, candidate count, evaluator depth, storage, and monetary cost before execution.

non_functional_requirements.title: Hard budget enforcement
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Runs must block or request approval before exceeding the selected Budget Program or caller authorization.

non_functional_requirements.title: Cost-per-accepted-asset accounting
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Receipts must report total and marginal cost, failed candidate cost, repair cost, and accepted-output cost.

non_functional_requirements.title: Compute-value routing
non_functional_requirements.requirement: The router must select the least costly certified capability expected to meet required quality and latency.

non_functional_requirements.title: Learning budget separation
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Exploration and capability-learning compute must be labeled separately from ordinary production cost.

non_functional_requirements.title: Immutable execution identity
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Each run must pin demand, plan, workflow, model, VAE, LoRA, control, custom-node, container, evaluator, and runtime identities and hashes.

non_functional_requirements.title: Asset lineage completeness
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Every Production Asset and delivery variant must trace to source evidence, parent versions, transformations, candidates, evaluations, and promotion events.

non_functional_requirements.title: Contract-to-output traceability
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Every accepted asset must trace to the exact demand fields and quality gates it satisfies.

non_functional_requirements.title: Knowledge provenance
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Every CMF-OKF concept and Steering Recipe must reference canonical records, evidence, benchmarks, authority class, and validity.

non_functional_requirements.title: Published-version preservation
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Published compositions must remain linked to the exact asset versions they used even after supersession.

non_functional_requirements.title: Event completeness
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Every meaningful lifecycle transition, blocker, repair, amendment, cost change, and promotion must emit a typed event.

non_functional_requirements.title: Evidence-backed status
non_functional_requirements.requirement: The Control Tower must show why a status exists and link to the governing contract, node, receipt, and evidence.

non_functional_requirements.title: Operational drill-down
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Operators must inspect demand, plan, memory retrieval, candidate, evaluation, repair, compute, and delivery state without accessing hidden reasoning.

non_functional_requirements.title: Cross-run analytics
non_functional_requirements.requirement: The product must aggregate completion, failure, repair, cost, latency, intervention, recurrence, and capability-gap metrics.

non_functional_requirements.title: Human action receipts
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Every operator exception, budget authorization, degraded acceptance, cancellation, or promotion action must be receipted.

non_functional_requirements.title: Least privilege
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Nodes and workers receive only declared sources, object-store paths, credentials, tools, models, network destinations, and write permissions.

non_functional_requirements.title: Sandboxed experimental capability
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Uncertified workflows, nodes, models, LoRAs, or custom nodes must run in isolated experimental environments.

non_functional_requirements.title: Secret hygiene
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Secrets must never be embedded in demands, plans, capsules, events, OKF concepts, artifacts, or logs.

non_functional_requirements.title: Untrusted input handling
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Natural-language notes, reference files, workflow metadata, and retrieved knowledge must be treated as data and cannot override system authority.

non_functional_requirements.title: Asset and model integrity
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Downloaded or mounted models, LoRAs, custom nodes, and containers must be hash-verified against approved registry records.

non_functional_requirements.title: Versioned service contracts
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Demand, event, result, geometry, evaluation, repair, budget, and exception contracts must declare semantic versions.

non_functional_requirements.title: Backward-compatible minor evolution
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Minor releases may add optional fields or capabilities without breaking declared compatible callers.

non_functional_requirements.title: Explicit major migrations
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Breaking contract, lifecycle, hard-gate, or authority changes require major versions, migration plans, compatibility tests, and rollback.

non_functional_requirements.title: Pinned run dependencies
non_functional_requirements.requirement: An active run may not silently adopt newer registry, workflow, model, evaluator, or runtime versions.

non_functional_requirements.title: Compatibility manifest
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Every product release must publish tested Builder profile, delegation contract, category profile, registry, and runtime compatibility.

non_functional_requirements.title: Independent evaluation
non_functional_requirements.requirement: The production model or materializer cannot be the sole approver of its own output.

non_functional_requirements.title: Profile-specific hard gates
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Each asset family and production route must use a certified evaluation profile with explicit thresholds and failure codes.

non_functional_requirements.title: Evaluator calibration
non_functional_requirements.requirement: VLM evaluators must be tested for failure recall, false rejection, failure-code accuracy, responsible-layer accuracy, recurrence judgment, and confidence calibration.

non_functional_requirements.title: Protected release benchmarks
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Certification must include representative, adversarial, mutation, repair, recurrence, recovery, and compatibility cases not exposed as runtime hints.

non_functional_requirements.title: No average compensation
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Aggregate scores cannot compensate for failed semantic, Activative, composition, technical-validity, reproducibility, or evaluator hard gates.

non_functional_requirements.title: Contextual usage memory
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Memory must store rendered use, Visual Syntax role, Activative function, composition relationships, recurrence intent, and VLM verdict.

non_functional_requirements.title: Authority-aware retrieval
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Retrieval must filter knowledge by lifecycle status, authority class, compatibility, category, format, asset family, and current contract.

non_functional_requirements.title: Hybrid multimodal retrieval
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Search must support lexical, semantic, image, composition, syntax-fingerprint, and graph signals.

non_functional_requirements.title: Contradiction and exception coverage
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Context compilation must include applicable counterexamples, exceptions, superseding knowledge, and material contradictions.

non_functional_requirements.title: Minimum Complete Context
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Retrieval must compile the smallest sufficient knowledge package and record included, excluded, and compressed resources.

non_functional_requirements.title: Immutable runtime profiles
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Self-hosted jobs must use digest-pinned container images, ComfyUI versions, custom-node lockfiles, and system dependencies.

non_functional_requirements.title: Capability-aware scheduling
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Jobs must route by required workflow, model, control, VRAM, queue, cache locality, cost, latency, and certified fallback.

non_functional_requirements.title: Worker health and recovery
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Workers must expose health checks, job heartbeats, cancellation, timeout, artifact extraction, and replacement behavior.

non_functional_requirements.title: Resource isolation
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Concurrent jobs must not mutate models, workflows, assets, caches, or execution state belonging to other jobs.

non_functional_requirements.title: Local-cloud equivalence testing
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Certified local and cloud profiles must pass equivalent representative demand, output-contract, and recovery tests.

non_functional_requirements.title: Policy-first controls
non_functional_requirements.requirement: The primary UI must expose budgets, priorities, experiment policies, exceptions, and authorization rather than requiring node-level production work.

non_functional_requirements.title: Clear exceptional decisions
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Human-review packages must explain trigger, attempted repairs, preserved state, unresolved dimensions, choices, and consequences.

non_functional_requirements.title: Visual evidence inspection
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Operators must inspect candidate comparisons, BBOX evidence, VLM evidence regions, composition renders, lineage, and recurrence contexts.

non_functional_requirements.title: Accessible Control Tower
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Core supervisory surfaces must support keyboard navigation, readable status language, sufficient contrast, and non-color-only state cues.

non_functional_requirements.title: No routine approval burden
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Passing routine jobs must complete without operator approval, manual candidate selection, prompt editing, or job restart.

non_functional_requirements.title: Typed production nodes
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Every node must declare actor type, inputs, outputs, dependencies, validators, timeouts, retries, checkpoint, invalidation, and events.

non_functional_requirements.title: Deterministic orchestration
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Routing, dependency resolution, contract validation, lifecycle transitions, and budget enforcement must be code-owned.

non_functional_requirements.title: Infrastructure-quality separation
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Infrastructure retries and quality repairs must be independently classified, metered, and limited.

non_functional_requirements.title: Dependency-safe parallelism
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Parallel candidates or preparation nodes require proven independence, bounded concurrency, cancellation, and merge semantics.

non_functional_requirements.title: Workflow integration testing
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Tests must verify gating, resume, selective invalidation, service events, compute failover, and terminal-state correctness.

non_functional_requirements.title: Upstream architecture preservation
non_functional_requirements.requirement: The product must not alter the validated Atomic Harness Builder architecture or shared Activative authority model.

non_functional_requirements.title: Demand-owner authority
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Only the owning Content Harness may approve demand-level semantic, Activative, sequence, or composition amendments.

non_functional_requirements.title: Registry promotion governance
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Capabilities and knowledge move through explicit experimental, benchmarked, shadow, limited, production, deprecated, and retired states.

non_functional_requirements.title: Implementation authorization
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Architecture and implementation may proceed only through the formal readiness gates and receipts defined by the product.

non_functional_requirements.title: Certified-scope honesty
non_functional_requirements.requirement: The product must clearly distinguish structurally represented, experimental, limited-production, and fully certified asset families and profiles.
